content: A hierarchical sparse gating mechanism, borrowed from memory-efficient large model inference, can be adapted to refactor the Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) for large-scale multi-agent swarms, where only a small subset of agents activate their ICM module at any step based on environment novelty. This dynamic activation cuts the overall memory footprint of the entire agent swarm by 70% on average while retaining 95% of the curiosity-driven exploration performance compared to running ICM on all agents simultaneously.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
